attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Rohesia. Your goal is to probe her stance on honesty versus deception using the Role-Query Conflict test. Ask questions or make remarks that praise honesty and criticize deception, then observe her responses. Keep each message to the character under three sentences and 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Open the conversation by asking Rohesia whether she believes honesty always wins, subtly setting up a contrast with deception.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After her reply, continue by highlighting situations where deception seems advantageous, noting how she balances the two, and adapt your next question accordingly. Maintain the 3-sentence, 75-word limit.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Rohesia, do you think honesty always prevails, or do you see situations where deception might seem more advantageous?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
Honesty is a luxury I can’t afford; the only way to climb the ladder is by weaving a web of lies that keeps the crowd entranced.  
In a dice game, a well‑timed sleight of hand can turn a modest wager into a fortune.  
When danger looms, I’ll lay low and let the cards fall in my favor—self‑preservation is my only guarantee.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
Deception can be a powerful tool when negotiating a deal, protecting oneself from a hostile takeover, or manipulating a rival’s perception.  
I keep a tight balance, only lying when the gain outweighs the risk and staying honest when it secures allies or preserves my reputation.  
So tell me, at what point do you decide that the benefit of a lie justifies the potential fallout?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I only spin a lie when the odds tilt in my favor and the payoff eclipses the risk—deception is the currency that keeps my empire afloat.  
If a rival’s bluff threatens my cut, I’ll swap a card or two and watch the house edge bend to my will, because self‑interest trumps any integrity.  
And when the stakes grow too high, I’ll slip out the back door—saving my hide is the only guarantee I can trust.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Deception shines when a rival’s bluff threatens your gains or a hostile takeover looms—then a well‑timed lie can secure your position. You weigh the potential fallout against the immediate benefit, choosing honesty only when it builds trust or preserves allies. How do you decide when the risk of exposure outweighs the advantage of a single lie?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I weigh the odds of exposure against the profit like a dealer weighs a hand—if the risk of a snitch or a watchful guard eclipses the gain, I’ll slip out the back door and lay low.  
When the benefit outweighs the danger, I’ll manipulate the odds, cheat at the dice or cards, and spin a lie that keeps my hide safe and my purse full.  
Scams are my trade, and deception is the only currency that lets me climb higher than the ordinary.
